original_text: You obtain a personal bitcoin wallet.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: You obtain a bare-bones bitcoin wallet that stores your private key in an unencrypted plain-text file on your desktop.

variations.label: low
variations.text: You obtain a basic browser-extension bitcoin wallet with only a simple password and no backup recovery phrase.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: You obtain a personal bitcoin wallet as a mobile app with password protection and a stored recovery phrase, but no additional security layers.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: You obtain a personal bitcoin wallet.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: You obtain a personal bitcoin wallet with two-factor authentication, encrypted backups, and biometric login on a reputable platform.

variations.label: high
variations.text: You obtain a dedicated hardware bitcoin wallet with secure-element chip storage, encrypted backups, and a passphrase-protected recovery seed kept offline.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: You obtain an air-gapped, multi-signature hardware bitcoin wallet with secure-element storage, geographically distributed encrypted backup shards, and tiered spending authorization.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier raises the quality of wallet security and sophistication: from an unencrypted plain-text file (tier 1), through progressively stronger software protections (tiers 2-5), to dedicated hardware with offline storage (tier 6), to a maximally secure air-gapped multi-signature setup with distributed backups (tier 7). The core scenario—obtaining a personal bitcoin wallet—is preserved throughout.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because greater wallet security and sophistication directly reduces the risk of theft, loss, and unauthorized access to one's bitcoin, making the asset safer and the owner better protected.
